授業目的: Students will understand how to use concepts and principles of politics and some economics, together with plausible evidence, to describe the interactions of political and economic actors and to explain the economic and political regime of the postwar Japan.
到達目標: Students will be able to understand the various ways to explain the economic growth of the postwar Japan.　They will also be able to discuss what is wrong and/or what went wrong since the 1990s.  Students will be able to understand the various ways to examine political regime of the postwar Japan.　They will also be able to think critically about competing theories of development （economists, sociologists, political scientists.
特記事項: 【合併】総合日本学習科目（政治経済）（CIEC）/Political Economy of Japan（国際学部）
授業方法: Upon reading the required reading, the students are expected to take in-class tests and participate in the class.  Each studnet is supposed to make one presentation on the reading in the semester.

第2回:
【Core Weeks①】
"Face-to-face class"／《90minutes》

PART I: POLITICAL ECONOMY: Developmental State?
Culture, Ideology
第3回:
【Core Weeks②】
"Face-to-face class"／《90minutes》

Dominant Explanations for the Political of Japan
第8回:
【Flex Hours①】
"Online class（Task-oriented format）"／《90minutes》

Read the article that I assigne and answer the questions in LUNA
第9回:
【Core Weeks⑦】
"Face-to-face class"／《90minutes》

PART II:PUBLIC POLICIES: Welfare State?
Public Policies in General
第10回:
【Core Weeks⑧】
"Face-to-face class"／《90minutes》

Developmental State or Welfare State?
トピック.授業外学習2: Readings are kept to a minimum so that students can reasonably be expected to complete them in time to participate actively in class.

教科書/Required texts0: Kozo Yamamura and Yasukichi Yasuba, eds., The Political Economy of Japan, volume １, The Domestic Transformation （Stanford University Press, 1987）. Andrew Gordon, ed., Postwar Japan as History （The University of California Press, 1993）. We will read some articles from the above books.  Other aritcles and chapter in books are also assigned.
参考文献・資料/Reference books0:
Steven Reed, Making Common Sense of Japan.
Ezra Vogel. Japan: Adaptive Communitarianism. 
Yasusuke Murakami. The Japanese Model of Political Economy.
Alfred Chandler, Jr. Government Versus Business: An American Phenomenon.
Gregory Noble.The Japanese Industrial Policy Debate.
Masahiko Aoki. The Japanese Firm in Transition.
Martin Kenney and Richard Florida. Beyond Mass Production 
Kazuo Koike. Human Resource Development and Labor-Management Relations.
Steven Vogel, aritlcles from Nikkei Shimbun
Michio Muramatsu and Ellis Krauss. The Conservative Policy Line and the Development of Patterned Pluralism．
Terry MacDougal. Democracy and Local Government in Postwar Japan.
Toshiya Kitayama, “The Welfare State in Japan,” in Koichiro Agata, Hiroaki Inatsugu, Hideaki Shiroyama eds., Public Administration in Japan, Palgrave Macmillan, 2024
Frank Upham. Unplaced Persons and Movemants for Place.
Margarita Estevez-Abe, “Gendering the Varieties of Capitalism: Gender Bias in Skills and Social Policies,”
Frances McCall Rosenbluth & Michael F. Thies, Japan Transformed: Political Change and Economic Restructuring
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: 全学統一様式による調査

各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1※kwicへのリンクとなります（在学生のみ閲覧可）
